ceiling.label: LiH
ceiling.interpretation: Chemical accuracy reachable across the scan.

ceiling.label: H₂O
ceiling.interpretation: Improves near threshold; useful graded RL progress.

ceiling.label: (H₂O)₂
ceiling.interpretation: Close but not chemical accuracy; pool/grid limiting.

ceiling.label: H₄
ceiling.interpretation: Strong improvement over base; stalls at 10+ mHa.

ceiling.label: N₂
ceiling.interpretation: Large improvement over base; far from chemical accuracy.

curriculum.note: Chemical accuracy reachable or solved-by-base. The dense energy-improvement signal is reliable, making these strong candidates for early PPO reward learning.
Improve over base but not to chemical accuracy with current pools. Progress is graded, so these are downweighted for initial PPO.
Strong base improvement, but error stalls above 10 mHa. Operator-pool or parameter work is needed, so these are best used as evaluation-only tasks.
